subject: Computer Networking
title: QUIC: UDP Meets Reliability
description: A 60-second lesson on QUIC: UDP Meets Reliability.

hook.text: TCP is reliable but slow to start. UDP is fast but unreliable. Google asked: can we have both? QUIC was born.

buildup.text: QUIC runs on UDP but builds in reliability, flow control, and encryption — essentially TCP plus TLS combined.

discovery.text: Connections start in a single round trip. No separate TCP handshake, then TLS handshake. Just one step.

twist.text: QUIC fixes head-of-line blocking. If one stream stalls, others keep flowing. TCP can't do that.

climax.text: HTTP/3 is built on QUIC. Chrome and major CDNs already use it for faster, more resilient web browsing.

punchline.text: QUIC is the next evolution of internet transport.

quiz.question: What problem does QUIC solve that TCP cannot?
